title: Audio-mastering adapter (loudness/peak/noise; FLAC→Opus/MP3)
context: Donated home recordings arrive with inconsistent loudness, potential clipping, and varying noise floors. The audio-mastering adapter normalizes audio to the spec's loudness target, enforces the true-peak ceiling, checks the noise floor, rejects clipped audio, retains the lossless FLAC master, and derives Opus and MP3 distribution files. It also emits a per-file quality report so downstream reviewers can see objective measurements before spending time on human QA. Depends on spec-001 (audio-quality spec) for its target values.
objective: Implement the audio-mastering adapter in adapters/audio/ that wraps ffmpeg and loudness-measurement tooling to normalize, check, and derive audio files per the audio-quality spec, emitting a per-file quality report.
acceptanceCriteria: Normalizes audio to the integrated-loudness target specified in spec-001 (EBU R128 / ITU-R BS.1770), enforces true-peak ≤ -1 dBTP, and checks that the noise floor is within the spec threshold.
Rejects (does not silently pass) audio that contains clipping; emits a clear rejection reason in the quality report.
Retains the lossless FLAC master and derives Opus and MP3 at the specified bitrates.
Emits a per-file quality report containing: integrated LUFS, true-peak dBTP, noise floor dBFS, duration, clipping flag, and pass/fail status against the spec.
Passes pnpm build and pnpm test (unit tests covering: correct report fields, rejection of clipped audio, loudness normalization within tolerance, format derivation); pnpm lint clean.
Licensed MIT; DCO sign-off on all commits.
resources: read-aloud-audio-spec-001 (audio-quality spec: LUFS target, true-peak ceiling, noise floor, formats)
output: A merged PR adding adapters/audio/ with the audio-mastering adapter implementation, unit tests, and documentation covering input requirements, output format paths, the quality report fields, and clipping-rejection behavior.
